text: Object tracking in video sequences is a fundamental problem that has numerous applications in computer vision and has therefore been studied extensively in the past few decades. An instance of this problem is robust multi-object tracking, which requires a deep understanding of spatial and temporal interactions among moving objects. In this paper, we design a novel tracking framework, called TransMOT, relying on graph transformers to model such complex interactions between objects. To handle the enormous number of objects that appear in a video sequence, we represent object trajectories by a set of sparse weighted graphs. Based on these graphs, our proposed framework is made up of a spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder and a spatial graph transformer decoder. TransMOT not only runs faster than conventional Transformers but also obtains more accurate tracking results by fully utilizing the spatial arrangement of objects in a scene. In addition, to avoid wasting resources on processing low-score detections and handling long-term occlusions, a cascade association framework is developed to alleviate the computational burden. Finally, experiment results on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17 and MOT20 show that our proposed method is state-of-the-art on all these four benchmarks.
